Petition To Establish Special Needs Trust For The Benefit Of Grant D. Shepard; Petition For Expanded Investment Powers; Petition For Authority To Pay
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nena Sheldon appeared by zoom for and with Katherine Chow (Guardian Ad Litem) by zoom.

The Court noted that the amendment was submitted and questions why language regarding the three percent increases (at home therapist/one on one school aid) was left in the amendment. Ms. Sheldon stated that what she understood from the last hearing was that the three percent increases would be limited until the first accounting. The Court stated that it wants to limit the Los Angeles Court's discretion as little as possible, that it will not approve the three percent increases and that the Los Angeles Court can decide this. Ms. Sheldon addressed the issue of the trust protector stating that Tanis L. Kelly is a licensed Attorney and a settlement consultant as well, and that Ms. Kelly does serve as a trust protector on other special needs trusts. The issue of the investments was also addressed. The Court stated that its concern is that it does not know what the expense ratios are for the investments and that it can allow the Los Angeles court to approve the investments, or not. Discussion was held regarding the prayer amount requested for the tuition fee being listed as $3,315.00 per month. Ms. Sheldon stated that it is very critical to have the special needs trust approved, that Grant has tremendous needs that only this special needs trust can supplement, and that Ms. Chow has depleted her savings. Ms. Chow stated that she really needs to Court to put this to bed and finish this out, that she liquidated her cash accounts yesterday, spoke of the hardship this has had on her, and spoke of the benefit in services the day care provides for Grant. The Court explained its role in making sure Grant's interests are protected and stated that it takes time to make sure that the terms of the special needs trust are in Grant's best interest. The Amended Petition to Establish Special Needs Trust is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Establish Special Needs Trust For The Benefit Of Grant D. Shepard; Petition For Expanded Investment Powers; Petition For Authority To Pay
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Continued To Oct-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nena Sheldon appeared by zoom for Petitioner.

Discussion was held regarding the Court's concerns with respect to the proposed order not specifically identifying the investments, the percentage of the annual costs to the trust for the Trustee, the Financial Advisor and the mutual funds, the amount of control/authority that will be given to Raymond James Trust (no separation of authority/too much authority to any one Trustee), the requirement of having a Trust Protector, language in Article 5.7 regarding replacement of a Trustee that reads "In the event there is no one then willing or able to serve as Successor Trustee, the Court shall designate the Successor Trustee." (the Court will not do so), the Court's position that it will not authorize the request to pay interim Trustee's fees out of the assets of the trust based on the Trustee's most recent published fee schedule, the Court's position that it will not approve an 18% increase in the school tuition, and that it won't approve a 3% increase for the one-on-one school aid at least not until the first accounting, etc. The Court stated that this is not going to be under this Court's supervision, that it will not tie another Court's hands, and that Ms. Sheldon can make the necessary revisions to the Court's satisfaction, or take this off calendar and file it in Los Angeles. Further discussion was held with regard to language/structure of a new proposed order and how it should read, the petition needing to be amended with a revised trust instrument to include a provision for a Trust Protector, sentences that are to be removed and/or changed in Article 5.7 and 5.8, other forms of investments that may be cheaper in costs, the Trust Protector not necessarily needing to be a professional fiduciary, inserting of language with regard to the need for Court approval if the investments need to change, etc. The Amended Petition to Establish Special Needs Trust is continued to October 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the amended petition with a revised trust instrument, and for a new proposed order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

Petition To Establish Special Needs Trust For The Benefit Of Grant D. Shepard; Petition For Expanded Investment Powers; Petition For Authority To Pay
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Continued To Aug-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nenaver Sheldon appeared by telephone for Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL").

The Court questions why this petition is here in this Court, how this Court has venue, and noted that the Proposed Trustee is located in Los Angeles County and the day to day administration of the trust will occur in Los Angeles County. The Court also stated that the request for an 18% increase per year for the pre-school tuition seems high, that the authority they are seeking with regard to the investments is too broad, that they need to be more specific as to the what the mutual funds will be and what types of bonds, and questions what Raymond James Trust's fees will be. Ms. Sheldon stated that the petition was filed here because the minor's compromise was filed here and approved by this Court, that the proposed Trustee is located in Los Angeles which is where the day to day administration of the trust will occur, that the proposed Beneficiary is located in San Francisco, that once the Special Needs Trust ("SNT") is established they will then petition for venue and that it is common that SNT's are established in whatever Court approved the minor's compromise. Ms. Sheldon also stated that Los Angeles has a procedure in place where a probate number gets assigned and OSC's are heard, and stated that powers and authorities will be addressed at the time when the first accounting is heard. In addition, Ms. Sheldon stated that the information regarding the pre-school tuition was received by the GAL, that given what is going on with the pandemic that will most likely change and may be less, that they will be able to show the Court of any increases and that they can petition to the Court. The Court stated that it will need more information regarding the tuition and that it will not build in any increases because it will not be reviewing or approving the accountings. Ms. Sheldon spoke of the Trustee's administrative fee rate as well as the investment management fee rate, and stated that investment management fees are fairly common in corporate trustees. The Court stated that the fees seem very high, suggested that they think more about the fees and see if they can get a better deal, stated that it wants to see different numbers or more support for those numbers, and raised the issue of the Attorney's fees with regard to research being related to the civil action. Ms. Sheldon stated that a significant amount of time was for communication with the medical malpractice firm regarding Grant's needs, the settlement, allocation of the minor's compromise matter and the order. Ms. Sheldon also stated that it takes time to address the language of the SNT, that they need to consider and discuss any potential issues that may come up with the government agencies, that the government agencies got notice, that they will also get notice when the SNT is approved, established and funded, and that they typically do not see government agencies come back and challenge the SNT's. In addition, Ms. Sheldon stated that she is confident that the Attorney's fees are valid and were a benefit to Grant Shepherd and to the SNT, that she went back and looked over the records regarding time spent on research and that it is was lower than what the Probate Examiner listed. The Petition to Establish Special Needs Trust for the Benefit of Grant D. Shepherd, etc. is continued to August 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a supplement regarding the intended investments and more information regarding the anticipated fees for Raymond James Trust, or an alternative Trustee. Ms. Sheldon is to provide a new proposed order taking out any indexed increases of expenditures. If any modest indexed increases are left in, the order needs to say that they must be limited in time until the first accounting.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
